title: crassula exilis subsp. cooperi
intro: Crassula exilis subsp. cooperi, commonly known as the 'Mini Jade', is a small succulent plant belonging to the Crassulaceae family. It is traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant is said to offer various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and providing antioxidant protection against cell damage. In traditional herbal preparations, the 'Mini Jade' is often used to make infusions, tinctures, or salves, which are applied topically to affected areas or taken internally to support overall well-being.
